title: On the Geometry and Arithmetic of Non-Spherical Maass Forms
speaker: Djordje Milicevic
abstract:
Central predictions of arithmetic quantum chaos such as the Quantum Unique Ergodicity conjecture and the sup-norm problem ask about the mass distribution of automorphic forms, most classically in terms of their weight or Laplace eigenvalue (for classical and Maass forms, respectively), but also in terms of the volume of the manifold and other parameters. The full automorphic spectrum on a reductive group G involves forms of all possible types under its maximal compact subgroup K, and it is natural to ask about the impact of the non-spherical spectrum (in particular, in the large dimension limit) on the mass concentration and spectral geometry on G.
In this walk, we will motivate the sup-norm problem and then describe our results, joint with Blomer, Harcos, and Maga, solving the problem for non-spherical Maass forms of an increasing dimension of the associated K-type in two settings, on arithmetic quotients of G=SL(2,C) and G=SL(3,R). We describe in detail the mass concentration of spherical trace functions of arbitrary K-type and are led to new counting problems of Hecke correspondences close to various special submanifolds of G.

proof_stages.label: Sup-norm problem and arithmetic quantum-chaos motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk introduces Laplace eigenfunctions/Maass forms, motivates pointwise sup-norm bounds via high-energy mass concentration, recalls classical spherical and holomorphic arithmetic bounds, and reframes them representation-theoretically.

proof_stages.label: Large K-type non-spherical problem for SL(2,C)
proof_stages.summary: For SL(2,C), the nonabelian maximal compact SU(2) gives higher-dimensional K-types τ_l, and the main SL(2,C) theorems are stated as subtrivial sup-norm bounds for lowest-weight non-spherical vectors with p=l large.

proof_stages.label: Pretrace formula and Hecke amplification reduction
proof_stages.summary: The proof strategy is reduced to choosing a geometric test function and Hecke amplifier that isolate the target automorphic form on the spectral side, leaving a counting problem for Hecke correspondences on the geometric side.

proof_stages.label: Non-spherical spherical-trace transform and SL(2,C) localization
proof_stages.summary: The key analytic input is the inverse spherical-trace transform for non-spherical K-types, whose concentration properties on SL(2,C) localize the counting to specific submanifolds rather than merely to the maximal compact.

proof_stages.label: SL(3,R) non-spherical trace-function formula
proof_stages.summary: The same method is set up for principal-series representations of SL(3,R) induced from the 2+1 parabolic, with an explicit K-integral formula for the relevant spherical trace function in the lowest K-type case.

proof_stages.label: SL(3,R) concentration hierarchy and final counting bound
proof_stages.summary: Analysis of the SL(3,R) trace function shows a hierarchy of concentration near the identity, a four-dimensional rotation-dilation manifold, and a six-dimensional shear enlargement, after which Hecke counting—partly via the pretrace formula used backwards—yields a sup-norm bound beating the baseline exponent by 1/40.
